title: Acute chest pain: differential and red-flag workup
key_recommendations: Use a structured pretest probability tool (HEART for ACS, Wells/PERC for PE) BEFORE committing to a single diagnostic pathway.
Pleuritic character, hypoxia, recent immobilization, and elevated D-dimer in any combination raise PE suspicion - CT pulmonary angiogram is the test of choice (V/Q if CT contraindicated).
Aortic dissection differentials: tearing pain radiating to back, BP differential between arms, mediastinal widening on CXR.
Troponin elevation does not exclude PE - right heart strain from PE can elevate troponin.
Avoid premature closure on the most-feared diagnosis. Imaging-first when probability is moderate.
